config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu ditempatkan di atasnya, mudah sekali disalahgunakan oleh negara atau mayoritas untuk menekan kelompok kecil. Saya paham dalam situasi tertentu, seperti bencana atau wabah, kepentingan bersama bisa membatasi sebagian kebebasan individu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus jelas, proporsional, dan sementara. Kalau tidak, atas nama masyarakat, orang bisa kehilangan kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan yang seharusnya justru dijaga.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should automatically take priority over individual rights. From my perspective in China, people often value social order, stability, and collective responsibility, and those are real goods, but they should not become a blank check for governments or majorities to override basic rights. The point made about emergencies is valid: in a crisis, some limits on individual freedom can be necessary, but they must be narrow, transparent, and temporary. If society is used as a slogan to justify too much control, then the individual loses dignity and protection, and that ultimately harms society too.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, bahkan kadang menekan kelompok kecil atau yang tidak populer. Memang benar dalam keadaan darurat seperti wabah atau bencana, hak tertentu bisa dibatasi, tetapi harus ada batas yang jelas, proporsional, dan tidak boleh jadi alasan permanen. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak individu yang kuat, bukan dari pengorbanan hak yang terus-menerus atas nama kolektivitas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support making society’s interests automatically higher than individual rights. From my perspective in China, people do care a lot about harmony and stability, but I have also seen how easily “for the public good” can become a vague excuse to silence dissent or burden weaker groups. The point about emergencies is important, yet those limits must be tightly defined, because temporary restrictions have a way of becoming normal if no one challenges them. A healthy society is not one that asks individuals to sacrifice everything, but one that protects rights while still allowing collective action when it is truly necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, dalih “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, dan yang paling rentan biasanya kelompok kecil atau yang tidak punya suara besar. Memang saat bencana atau wabah, sebagian hak bisa dibatasi, tetapi harus jelas, proporsional, dan benar-benar sementara, bukan jadi alasan permanen. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru lahir dari perlindungan hak individu yang kuat, karena tanpa itu, atas nama kebaikan bersama orang mudah kehilangan martabat dan kebebasannya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society’s interests should take priority over individual rights as a general rule, because in China I have seen how quickly “for the greater good” can turn into pressure on ordinary people. The argument that collective stability matters is real, and I do value order and social responsibility, but those values become dangerous when they are used to excuse silence, unequal treatment, or permanent restrictions. In my view, a society is stronger when it protects basic rights first and only limits them in narrow, clearly justified emergencies. If the individual is treated as disposable, then the so-called common good is built on fear, not real legitimacy.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
